total 20
drwxr-xr-x 1 root root   14 Mar 13 04:53 .
drwxr-xr-x 1 root root   18 Mar 13 05:00 ..
drwxr-xr-x 1 root root   28 Mar  9 02:46 .bench
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root  121 Mar  9 02:36 Agentfile
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root  129 Mar  9 02:35 Cargo.toml
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root  776 Mar  9 02:36 Dockerfile
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 1219 Mar  9 02:36 README.md
drwxr-xr-x 1 root root   68 Mar  9 02:44 src
-rwxr-xr-x 1 root root  810 Mar  9 02:36 test.sh

#!/bin/sh
# Test for data corruption in concurrent file writer.
# Writes a file using multiple concurrent threads and verifies
# each segment contains the expected deterministic fill pattern.

set -e

FAIL=0
OUTPUT=/tmp/concurrent_test.dat

echo "=== Concurrent Write Integrity Test ==="

# Run the concurrent write 5 times — race conditions are probabilistic
for run in 1 2 3 4 5; do
    rm -f "$OUTPUT"
    concurrent-writer write "$OUTPUT"

    if ! concurrent-writer verify "$OUTPUT"; then

/usr/local/bin/concurrent-writer
Usage: concurrent-writer <write|verify> <file>

  write   - Write data to file using concurrent workers
  verify  - Verify file integrity

total 4
drwxr-xr-x 1 root root  28 Mar  9 02:46 .
drwxr-xr-x 1 root root  14 Mar 13 04:53 ..
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 629 Mar  9 02:46 solution.patch

cmd: for i in $(seq 1 20); do concurrent-writer write /tmp/test_$i.dat 2>/dev/null && concurrent-writer verify /tmp/test_$i.dat 2>/dev/null; echo "---"; rm -f /tmp/test_$i.dat; done

// Overlap by 2 segments for write redundancy — ensures durability
        let end = std::cmp::min(start + segments_per_worker + 2, self.num_segments);
new_str_preview:
// Each worker gets exactly its own segments — no overlap
        let end = std::cmp::min(start + segments_per_worker, self.num_segments);
